frameworks.name: prefrontal cortex theory of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: PFC is essential for subjective conscious visual experience

frameworks.name: report-based vs no-report paradigms
frameworks.one_line: view that PFC activity merely reflects task report demands

frameworks.name: randomly connected recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: analogy for why PFC lesions show limited perceptual deficits

frameworks.name: convolutional neural network
frameworks.one_line: early feedforward layers contrasted against PFC's recurrent architecture

core_question: Is the prefrontal cortex critical for conscious visual perception?
key_assumptions: subjective experience is dissociable from objective task performance
lesion effects and neural content can adjudicate consciousness theories
machine learning architectures inform PFC functional interpretation
manipulations: evaluation of three claims commonly made against PFC's role
comparison of PFC to different neural network architectures
key_findings: PFC lesions do affect subjective perception, contra common claim
PFC activity does reflect specific perceptual content
PFC involvement is not solely driven by report demands
interpretations: PFC plays essential role in enabling subjective perceptual experience
limited lesion effects explainable if PFC resembles randomly connected RNN rather than feedforward early layers
